note: Captured under --quick at 1s/1s. Re-run without --quick for production numbers; the *ratios* between engines are stable across run lengths.

observations.wall_clock_at_realistic_repo_scale: Single 10k-line, 1000-function file: ~93ms semantic vs ~10ms text. A 100-file rebase touches ~10-30 files of this size; total bench-extrapolated overhead is sub-second.

follow_ups: Parser cost (tree-sitter) dominates >40% of wall-clock time at 1000 fns. Follow-up: investigate `SemanticParseCache` integration so a single tree-sitter parse can be amortized across `--with-diff` post-merge display and the merge itself.
